title: craibiodendron scleranthum var. scleranthum
intro: Craibiodendron scleranthum var. scleranthum, also known as the Taiwan Craibiodendron or Taiwan mountain apple, is a species of plant in the family Staphylocactaceae. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, colds, and sore throats. The leaves and roots of Craibiodendron scleranthum var. scleranthum contain antioxidants and flavonoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, providing health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Craibiodendron scleranthum var. scleranthum include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
